reference: Hi Linda, welcome to Health Care Management. Since you have stones in the kidney, liver, and spleen, you will need to do thyroid function tests to rule out hypercalcemia, which is a condition in which there are above-normal levels of calcium in the blood, which can lead to stone formation in various organs. If hypercalcemia is detected, then it will need to be treated. Liver and spleen stones do not need to be treated. If there are stones in the gallbladder and if they are causing problems, then surgical removal of the gallbladder is required. I wish you all the best.
text: Hi Linda, welcome to Health for More. Since we have stones in the kidney, liver, and spleen, you may see thyroid function tests tell you about hypercalcemia, which is a condition in which there are red monoledges of calcium and blood, which leads to cell formation in various organs. If hypercalcemia is the case, then it will need to be treated. Liver and spleen stones do not need to be treated. If there are stones in the gallbladder, and if there are causing problems, then a physical interview with the gallbladder is required. Have a good day.
